solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        _when N < 3 -> 0;
        _ ->
            % 1. Calculate Costs C[i] for i = 1 to N-2
            Costs = calculate_costs(Nums, N),
            
            N_prime = N - 2,
            K_max = (N_prime + 1) div 2,

            INF = 1000000000000000000, % Sufficiently large number for Infinity

            % Initialization: Before i=1
            % S_prev[0] = 0, S_prev[k>0] = INF
            S_prev_list = [0 | list_of_size(K_max, INF)],
            % D_prev[k] = INF for all k
            D_prev_list = list_of_size(K_max + 1, INF),
            
            % 2. DP Iteration
            {D_final, S_final} = dp_iterate(Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_prev_list, S_prev_list),

            % 3. Result
            Result = min(D_final !! K_max, S_final !! K_max),
            Result
    end.

calculate_costs(Nums, N) ->
    % Costs list indexed 1 to N-2. Index 0 unused.
    Costs = [0 | list_of_size(N - 2, 0)],
    
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, i) ->
        % i is the peak index (1 <= i <= N-2)
        
        % Nums is 0-indexed, so neighbors are at i-1 and i+1
        
        N_i_minus_1 = Nums !! (i - 1),
        N_i_plus_1 = Nums !! (i + 1),
        N_i = Nums !! i,
        
        Required_Value = max(N_i_minus_1, N_i_plus_1) + 1,
        
        Cost = max(0, Required_Value - N_i),
        
        lists:replace(Acc, i, Cost)
    end, Costs, 1 .. N - 2).

% dp_iterate(Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_prev, S_prev) -> {D_final, S_final}
dp_iterate(Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_prev, S_prev) ->
    % Helper function to perform the iterative DP step
    dp_loop(1, Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_prev, S_prev).

dp_loop(i, Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_prev, S_prev) when i > N_prime ->
    {D_prev, S_prev};

dp_loop(i, Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_prev, S_prev) ->
    C_i = Costs !! i,
    
    % Initialize current state arrays
    S_curr_list = list_of_size(K_max + 1, INF),
    D_curr_list = list_of_size(K_max + 1, INF),

    % Calculate S_curr and D_curr iteratively for k = 0 to K_max
    {S_curr, D_curr} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, k) ->
        
        % 1. Calculate S_curr[k] (Skip i)
        S_k = min(D_prev !! k, S_prev !! k),
        
        % 2. Calculate D_curr[k] (Select i)
        D_k = 
            if k > 0 ->
                % Must have skipped i-1. Look at S_prev[k-1]
                S_prev_k_minus_1 = S_prev !! (k - 1),
                if S_prev_k_minus_1 == INF ->
                    INF
                else
                    S_prev_k_minus_1 + C_i
                end
            else
                INF % Cannot select i if k=0
            end,
        
        % Update the accumulator list for k
        lists:replace(Acc, k, {S_k, D_k})
    end, {S_curr_list, D_curr_list}, 0 .. K_max),

    % Prepare for next iteration
    dp_loop(i + 1, Costs, N_prime, K_max, INF, D_curr, S_curr).

% --- Helper Functions ---

% Helper to create a list of specified size initialized with a value
list_of_size(Size, Value) ->
    lists:duplicate(Value, Size).

% Helper function to perform minimum of two terms
min(A, B) ->
    if A < B -> A;
    true -> B
    end.
```

    N = length(Nums),
    if N < 3 ->
        0;
    true =

        Costs = [
            C | RestCosts
        ],
        C =
            case N of
                3 ->
                    % Only one potential peak i=1

                        [C_1]

            end,

        % Since Erlang doesn't easily support dynamic array resizing or complex loops like JS,
        % we generate the list of costs first.
        Costs = lists:map(
            fun(i) ->
                % i here is the index 1 to N-2
                C_i = max(0, max(Nums !! (i-1), Nums !! (i+1)) + 1 - Nums !! i),
                C_i
            end,
            lists:seq(1, N-2)
        ),

        % Step 2: Determine K_max
        N_prime = N - 2,
        K_max = (N_prime + 1) div 2, % Equivalent to ceil(N_prime / 2)

        % Step 3: Dynamic Programming
        INF = 1000000000000000000, % Sufficiently large number for infinity

        % Initialize DP states for i=0 (conceptual start)

        % Initial state: k=0, cost 0. All others INF.
        InitialState = lists:replicate(K_max + 1, INF),
        D_prev = lists:replace(InitialState, 0, 0),

        % Loop through potential peak indices i = 1 to N-2.

        {D_final, S_final} = lists:foldl(

solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    if N < 3 ->
        0;
    true =
        % Step 1: Calculate costs C[i] for i = 1 to N-2
        Costs = [
            C | RestCosts
        ],
        C =
            case N of
                3 ->
                    % Only one potential peak i=1
                    [1] ->
                        C_1 = max(0, max(Nums !! 0, Nums !! 2) + 1 - Nums !! 1),
                        [C_1]
                    _ ->
                        []; % Should not happen if N >= 3

            end,

        % Since Erlang doesn't easily support dynamic array resizing or complex loops like JS,
        % we generate the list of costs first.
        Costs = lists:map(
            fun(i) ->
                % i here is the index 1 to N-2
                C_i = max(0, max(Nums !! (i-1), Nums !! (i+1)) + 1 - Nums !! i),
                C_i
            end,
            lists:seq(1, N-2)
        ),

        % Step 2: Determine K_max
        N_prime = N - 2,
        K_max = (N_prime + 1) div 2, % Equivalent to ceil(N_prime / 2)

        % Step 3: Dynamic Programming
        INF = 1000000000000000000, % Sufficiently large number for infinity

        % Initialize DP states for i=0 (conceptual start)
        % D_prev[k]: Min cost ending at i-1, where i-1 WAS selected, having k peaks.
        % S_prev[k]: Min cost ending at i-1, where i-1 WAS skipped, having k peaks.
        
        % Initial state: k=0, cost 0. All others INF.
        InitialState = lists:replicate(K_max + 1, INF),
        D_prev = lists:replace(InitialState, 0, 0),
        S_prev = lists:replace(InitialState, 0, 0),

        % Loop through potential peak indices i = 1 to N-2.
        % We use fold/lists:foldl to iterate and update the states.
        {D_final, S_final} = lists:foldl(
            fun({D_prev, S_prev}, I) ->
                % I is the list of costs, index 0 corresponds to i=1, index k corresponds to i=k+1
                Ci = Costs !! (I - 1), % Cost for current index i
                
                % Initialize current states
                D_curr = lists:replicate(K_max + 1, INF),
                S_curr = lists:replicate(K_max + 1, INF),
                
                % Calculate D_curr and S_curr based on D_prev and S_prev
                {D_next, S_next} = lists:foldl(
                    fun({D_curr, S_curr}, K) ->
                        
                        % Case 1: Skip index i (S_curr[k])
                        S_curr_k = min(D_prev !! K, S_prev !! K),
                        S_curr_updated = lists:replace(S_curr, K, S_curr_k),
                        
                        % Case 2: Select index i (D_curr[k])
                        D_curr_k =
                            if K > 0 ->
                                % Must have skipped i-1. Need k-1 peaks from i-2.
                                if S_prev !! (K - 1) < INF ->
                                    S_prev !! (K - 1) + Ci
                                else
                                    INF
                            else
                                INF
                            end,
                        D_curr_updated = lists:replace(D_curr, K, D_curr_k),
                        
                        {D_curr_updated, S_curr_updated}
                    end,
                    {D_curr, S_curr},
                    lists:seq(0, K_max)
                ),
                
                {D_next, S_next}
            end,
            {D_prev, S_prev},
            lists:seq(1, N_prime)
        ),

        % Step 4: Result
        min(D_final !! K_max, S_final !! K_max).

min(A, B) -> B.

solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        N when N < 3 -> 0;
        _ ->
            % 1. Calculate costs C_i for i = 1 to N-2. 
            % Costs list C[j] corresponds to potential peak index i = j + 1.
            Costs = calculate_costs(Nums, N),
            
            N_prime = N - 2,
            
            case rem(N_prime, 2) of
                0 -> % N' is even. Check two sequences (start indices 0 and 1).
                    CostA = calculate_sum_indices(Costs, 0), 
                    CostB = calculate_sum_indices(Costs, 1), 
                    min(CostA, CostB);
                1 -> % N' is odd. Unique sequence (start index 0).
                    calculate_sum_indices(Costs, 0)
            end.

%% Helper function to calculate C_i for all i = 1 to N-2
calculate_costs(Nums, N) ->
    % We iterate i from 1 to N-2. The resulting list C has length N-2.
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        % I is the index in nums we are checking (1-based)
        % Since we are using lists:foldl over range, we need to map I to 0-based list access.
        
        % Get nums[i-1], nums[i], nums[i+1] (0-based indices: i-1, i, i+1)
        % Nums is 1-based in logic, but lists:foldl usually iterates over standard indices.
        
        % We use lists:nth for safety, although performance might suffer slightly compared to direct array access.
        A = lists:nth(I-1, Nums),
        B = lists:nth(I, Nums),
        C_val = lists:nth(I+1, Nums),

        % Required value = max(A, C_val) + 1
        RequiredValue = max(A, C_val) + 1,
        
        % Cost = max(0, RequiredValue - B)
        Cost = max(0, RequiredValue - B),
        
        [Cost | Acc]
    end, [], 1 .. N - 2).


%% Helper function to sum elements of Costs list C starting at index 'start_index'
calculate_sum_indices(Costs, StartIndex) ->
    % Costs is a list C[0], C[1], ..., C[N'-1]
    
    sum_list(Costs, StartIndex, 0).

sum_list([], _, Acc) -> Acc;
sum_list([H|T], StartIndex, Acc) ->
    case rem(StartIndex, 2) of
        0 ->
            % Current element matches the required parity
            NextStartIndex = StartIndex + 2,
            sum_list(T, NextStartIndex, Acc + H);
        _ ->
            % Current element does not match the required parity
            NextStartIndex = StartIndex + 2,
            sum_list(T, NextStartIndex, Acc)
    end.

solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    case N of
        N when N < 3 -> 0;
        _ ->
            % 1. Calculate costs C_i for i = 1 to N-2. 
            % Costs list C[j] corresponds to potential peak index i = j + 1.
            Costs = calculate_costs(Nums, N),
            
            N_prime = N - 2,
            
            case rem(N_prime, 2) of
                0 -> % N' is even. Check two sequences (start indices 0 and 1).
                    CostA = calculate_sum_indices(Costs, 0), 
                    CostB = calculate_sum_indices(Costs, 1), 
                    min(CostA, CostB);
                1 -> % N' is odd. Unique sequence (start index 0).
                    calculate_sum_indices(Costs, 0)
            end.

%% Helper function to calculate C_i for all i = 1 to N-2
calculate_costs(Nums, N) ->
    % We iterate i from 1 to N-2. The resulting list C has length N-2.
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        % I is the index in nums we are checking (1-based)
        % Since we are using lists:foldl over range, we need to map I to 0-based list access.
        
        % Get nums[i-1], nums[i], nums[i+1] (0-based indices: i-1, i, i+1)
        % Nums is 1-based in logic, but lists:foldl usually iterates over standard indices.
        
        % We use lists:nth for safety, although performance might suffer slightly compared to direct array access.
        A = lists:nth(I-1, Nums),
        B = lists:nth(I, Nums),
        C_val = lists:nth(I+1, Nums),

        % Required value = max(A, C_val) + 1
        RequiredValue = max(A, C_val) + 1,
        
        % Cost = max(0, RequiredValue - B)
        Cost = max(0, RequiredValue - B),
        
        [Cost | Acc]
    end, [], 1 .. N - 2).


%% Helper function to sum elements of Costs list C starting at index 'start_index'
calculate_sum_indices(Costs, StartIndex) ->
    % Costs is a list C[0], C[1], ..., C[N'-1]
    
    sum_list(Costs, StartIndex, 0).

sum_list([], _, Acc) -> Acc;
sum_list([H|T], StartIndex, Acc) ->
    case rem(StartIndex, 2) of
        0 ->
            % Current element matches the required parity
            NextStartIndex = StartIndex + 2,
            sum_list(T, NextStartIndex, Acc + H);
        _ ->
            % Current element does not match the required parity
            NextStartIndex = StartIndex + 2,
            sum_list(T, NextStartIndex, Acc)
    end.

```
